channel.title: Latest alerts from washingtonpost.com
channel.description: An unofficial feed created by Derek Willis.

item.title: The Trump administration is cracking down on dissent. That’s chilling.
item.description: The Trump administration is cracking down on dissent. That’s chilling.

item.title: Suspect in shooting of two National Guard troops in D.C. is hospitalized after refusing food, water
item.description: Rahmanullah Lakanwal, the man accused of shooting the troops last fall, killing one of them, was admitted to George Washington University Hospital and is in “dire circumstances,” a federal judge said at an emergency court hearing.

item.title: A sister who cut me out summoned me to care for her on her deathbed: Read the answer in our new advice column
item.description: A sister who cut me out summoned me to care for her on her deathbed: Read the answer in our new advice column

item.title: Here’s what a sleep expert wants you to eat every day for better rest
item.description: This professor’s research has found that certain foods are associated with deep, more restorative sleep.

item.title: Graham Platner’s small hometown will never see him the same way
item.description: Residents of Sullivan, Maine, are left to ponder what it means to enter the maelstrom of national politics, and what might be exposed along the way.

item.title: Ex-Olympian accused of Reflecting Pool vandalism pleads not guilty
item.description: Former Olympic canoeist David Hearn was indicted on one count of malicious destruction of property, a felony under D.C. law, in what his lawyers call the government’s “concocted” account.

item.title: ‘Widow’s Bay’ became a hit the old-fashioned way
item.description: Word of mouth, not the algorithm, turned Apple TV's horror-comedy into appointment viewing.

item.title: This senator is the focus of today’s On the Record
item.description: This senator is the focus of today’s On the Record

item.title: Secretary of State Marco Rubio tries to enlist other nations in antifa fight, but some allies recoil
item.description: The secretary of state plans to convene foreign ministers in Washington next week. The event has unsettled some U.S. and European officials.

item.title: Iran strikes. Platner’s exit. ICE shooting. Antifa. Cancer rates. Boomer wealth. Better sleep. Catch up now.
item.description: The 7.

item.title: Democrat Graham Platner ends campaign for U.S. Senate in Maine following allegation of sexual assault
item.description: The populist political newcomer, who Democrats had hoped could flip a critical U.S. Senate seat, ended his campaign Wednesday night after a woman he previously dated publicly accused him of sexual assault. Party leaders have until July 27 to decide on a replacement candidate to take on GOP Sen. Susan Collins in one of the marquee races of the midterm elections.

item.title: Boomers’ massive wealth will mostly be passed down to people who are already rich
item.description: Often called the Great Wealth Transfer, the inheritances are expected to be the largest passage of generational wealth in history.

item.title: Son of Mexican immigrant killed by ICE in Houston describes anguished search for his father
item.description: The eldest child of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo said his father was “a family man” who was likely frightened by the unmarked vehicles following him. Immigration and Customs Enforcement “did not present itself in an orderly manner,” Ronaldo Salgado said to reporters.

item.title: What a viral photo of white supremacists on the Metro reveals
item.description: What a viral photo of white supremacists on the Metro reveals

item.title: Smithsonian leader’s memo to staff disputes White House report that accused museum of ‘extreme political activism’
item.description: Smithsonian Secretary Lonnie G. Bunch III defended the work of the National Museum of American History, according to an email obtained by The Washington Post.

item.title: Graham Platner’s campaign staff was told he’ll speak tonight on the future of his Senate bid in Maine, Democrat close to him says
item.description: Platner could drop out of the race as early as today, probably by prerecorded video, said a second person close to Platner’s team.

item.title: 7 surprises and snubs from the Emmy nominations, including Zendaya, Jeremy Allen White and the actress who waged a solo campaign
item.description: “The Pitt” and “Hacks” dominated the field, but there was plenty that was unexpected in Wednesday’s announcement.

item.title: Who vetted Graham Platner? Anyone?
item.description: Democrats wanted a fighter badly enough to sacrifice their standards.

item.title: Idaho mother charged with murder said flu shots caused twins’ deaths
item.description: Andrea Shaw, a plaintiff in a lawsuit against the American Academy of Pediatrics for recommending childhood vaccines, is accused of suffocating her daughter and son.

item.title: A reader rants about an overcooked $25 burger and terrible service. Chat with our food critic now.
item.description: Washington Post food critic Elazar Sontag entertains your restaurant questions, rants and raves.

item.title: In shift, Trump praises Zelensky, says he will let Ukraine build Patriot missiles
item.description: President Donald Trump’s message marked a dramatic departure from his more acerbic tone toward Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky, whom he once derided as ungrateful.

item.title: Fill in the blank in today’s On the Record
item.description: Fill in the blank in today’s On the Record

item.title: Cancer cases worldwide will soar in the coming decades, a report finds. Here’s why.
item.description: The World Health Organization tempered optimism about improvements in cancer surveillance and treatment and warned that global health care inequities are driving further cases and deaths.

item.title: Iran strikes. Graham Platner. ICE shooting. Mitch McConnell. Trump’s arch. Tax refunds. Summer flying. Catch up now.
item.description: The 7.

item.title: Trump says ceasefire with Iran is over, calling its leaders 'scum'
item.description: The president declared an end to the truce at a NATO summit in Turkey after the U.S. and Iran exchanged dozens of strikes overnight, raising the prospect of major renewed hostilities.

item.title: ICE officer fatally shoots Mexican man in Houston during attempted immigration arrest, federal authorities say
item.description: Immigration and Customs Enforcement said the man, whom it identified as Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, was in the U.S. illegally. The incident appears to be the first fatal shooting involving federal immigration agents since U.S. citizens Renée Good and Alex Pretti were killed in separate incidents in Minneapolis in January.

item.title: A photo of a Black woman surrounded by members of a white-supremacist group went viral. Her family says she’s more than a symbol.
item.description: The Reuters photo shows the woman seated alone on a Metro train on the Fourth of July among masked members of Patriot Front, who were marching by the hundreds through D.C. that day. The image has been widely dissected online, some likening it to photos once seen during the civil rights movement and others using it to call out extremism in modern-day America.

item.title: Ex-girlfriend of Graham Platner says he removed condoms without consent during sex
item.description: Lyndsey Fifield, who said she dated Platner from 2013 to 2015 in D.C. and has previously accused him of physical abuse, is the second woman to allege this week that the Democratic nominee for Senate in Maine had engaged in unwanted sexual conduct.

item.title: Trump weighed in on Cabinet squabble over ride on fighter jet for July Fourth flyover
item.description: The president encouraged acting attorney general Todd Blanche to fly on one of NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman’s jets despite the FAA’s prior objection to the planes.

item.title: This little blue dot on your phone is revolutionary
item.description: U.S. policy used to jam up GPS. Now, those signals beam into your pocket.

item.title: As rumors about Mitch McConnell swirl, GOP leaders say they've spoken with the hospitalized senator
item.description: McConnell has not spoken publicly, and his office has not disclosed what sent him to the hospital June 14, leading right-wing influencers to spread unverified theories.

item.title: Trans people are fleeing red states for Seattle. The city can’t keep up.
item.description: Though trans people make up just 1 percent of the population in Washington state, the nonprofits that help them say their budgets are drained and their staffs are stretched so thin that last month the Seattle LGBTQ Commission asked Mayor Katie Wilson (D) to declare a civil state of emergency.

item.title: In Say More, our new relationship advice column, a letter writer is too embarrassed to tell their spouse about using Wegovy
item.description: A reader asks: Should I keep hiding my weight-loss pills — and the money I spend on them — and hope my husband “just thinks I’m dieting again”?

item.title: Bernie Sanders, a key supporter of Graham Platner, calls on the Democratic candidate to drop his bid for U.S. Senate in Maine
item.description: “I have spoken with Graham Platner about the best path forward for Maine,” Sanders said in a statement. “In light of these very serious allegations, I have recommended that he step aside.”

item.title: Goodbye, DOGE
item.description: It turns out that a commission based on a meme is not the solution to federal overspending.

item.title: A phone call in today’s On the Record
item.description: A phone call in today’s On the Record

item.title: If Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner drops out in the next six days, Democrats have a chance to pick his replacement
item.description: Platner faces a sexual assault allegation from a woman he previously dated and has seen his support from key Democrats collapse amid mounting pressure to withdraw before a July 13 deadline.

item.title: Graham Platner. World Cup. Mitch McConnell. Deportations. Smithsonian report. Supplements. Sleep divorce. Catch up now.
item.description: The 7.

item.title: U.S. falls to Belgium, 4-1, ending tantalizing World Cup run with defensive miscues
item.description: The Red Devils ended the Americans’ captivating run through the tournament in the round of 16, denying them their first trip to the World Cup quarterfinals since 2002. Belgium advanced to face Spain in Friday’s quarterfinal in Inglewood, California.

item.title: Woman accuses Graham Platner, Democratic Senate nominee in Maine, of sexually assaulting her
item.description: A woman who said she previously dated Platner, the 41-year-old ex-Marine and Democratic nominee in Maine, alleged in interviews that he sexually assaulted her in her home near the end of 2021. In a video posted on X on after the allegation surfaced, Platner denied wrongdoing but said “we are taking the time to reflect on the best path forward for the state that I love, the people that I love, the movement I belong to, and the goal of defeating Susan Collins.”

item.title: Our reporter tried Gwyneth Paltrow’s Goop Kitchen for a week. Here’s what happened.
item.description: Ashley Fetters Maloy writes that eating Goop’s grub left her feeling clean, powerful, smug and exasperated.

item.title: What actually causes cancer, and why has it become so common? An oncologist explains.
item.description: While more people are being diagnosed with cancer today compared with 50 years ago, the good news is that fewer people are dying of it.

item.title: China test-fires long-range missile into Pacific, rattling U.S. allies
item.description: The unprecedented test of a nuclear-capable submarine-launched missile highlights Beijing’s growing force in the region.

item.title: After he wrote a scathing message to ICE, federal agents showed up at his door. Now he’s suing.
item.description: David Streever of Rochester, New York, sued Department of Homeland Security officials on Monday, saying the agency’s actions violated his First Amendment rights.

item.title: Carolyn Hax: Tell kids that ex’s betrayal means ‘I no longer believe in love’?
item.description: A divorced letter writer doesn’t want to lie to the kids when they ask how things are with a new girlfriend.

item.title: Trump rushes White House helipad project ahead of visit by China’s president, adding $875,000 and requiring 24/7 work, records show
item.description: The contractor has sought to shave off more than a month of the planned construction timeline, documents show.

item.title: Trump says he asked FIFA’s president to review U.S. star’s World Cup suspension but ‘didn’t tell him what to do’
item.description: President Donald Trump’s involvement in obtaining a reprieve for Folarin Balogun has fueled concerns about political influence and cast a pall over Monday night’s match against Belgium.

item.title: White House report accuses Smithsonian museum of ‘extreme political activism’ and erasing nation’s heritage
item.description: The 162-page report on the National Museum of American History marks an escalation of President Donald Trump’s criticism of the Smithsonian museums.

item.title: Europe wants tourists out — except this kind
item.description: Restrictions on short-term rentals and other limitations are turning international travel into a luxury.

item.title: An NHL player stars in today’s On the Record
item.description: An NHL player stars in today’s On the Record
